LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITION TO DETERMINE THE STATUS OF WESTMINSTER PROJECTS, INCORPORATED AS A RELIGIOUS CORPORATION AND TO APPOINT DIRECTORS TO WIND UP THE AFFAIRS OF THE CORPORATION. GRANT. NO OPPOSITION FILED, AND GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. COUNSEL FOR THE PETITIONER IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THAT THE PETITION WAS NOT OPPOSED AND MAKES THE REQUESTED FINDINGS AND MUST EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Hearing On Petition To Determine The Status Of Westminster Projects, Incorporated As A Religious Corporation And To Appoint Directors To Wind Up The Affairs Of The Corporation
Off Calendar Aug-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Hearing On Petition To Determine The Status Of Westminster Projects, Incorporated As A Religious Corporation And To Appoint Directors To Wind Up The Affairs Of The Corporation.

Grant. No opposition filed, and good cause shown. Counsel for the Petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the petition was not opposed and makes the requested findings and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Determine Status Of Westminster Projects, Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-27-2020 Off Calendar
